_comment: Sweep registry. Each entry is one logical sweep — a named, reproducible set of Harbor trials that together produce a single canonical score for a (model, effort, substrate) configuration. When a sweep proceeds in chunks or includes correction re-runs, every component job directory is listed here so a future analyst can reconstruct exactly which trial counts for each task.

_selection_rules.latest_per_task: For each canonical task, pick the trial with the latest started_at timestamp whose job_dir matches any listed component glob. Use bench/results/oracle-tasks.json as the canonical task list.
_selection_rules.correction_override: Component list is ordered (seq 1, 2, …). For each canonical task, use the result from the highest-seq component whose job_dir_glob matches a directory containing a result for that task. If only one component covers the task, use that. This lets a later correction component supersede the main sweep for specific tasks without changing the rest.

sweeps.reconstruction_note: Score 53/89 was recorded at the time of the sweep but the exact trial-to-task mapping has not been preserved. The sweep was run across many job directories in parallel; known components are listed below, but later retry/supplementary runs (also listed) mean that 'latest_per_task' over all components yields ~62/89, not 53/89. A fresh n=1 replication is required for a reliable comparison against S2. The ~4 setup-timeouts mentioned in the README were caused by parallel Docker resource contention making container install steps time out; the exact 4 task names are not reliably recoverable.

components.label: phase-A batch 1
components.notes: First parallel batch. Some tasks hit setup timeouts due to resource contention.

components.label: phase-A retries
components.notes: Retry runs for tasks that failed or were missing in batch 1.

components.label: fix-e validation / fix-f polyglot / fix-f smoke
sonnet missing-13 gap fill
components.notes: Gap-fill run to cover the 13 canonical tasks absent from earlier batches.

sweeps.reconstruction_note: All 89 tasks are covered. For 87 tasks use component 1 (job dirs match v0115-seq-*-sonnet-medium-repl-centric). For fix-code-vulnerability use component 2; for pytorch-model-recovery use component 3. The as-run score of 50/89 is component 1 alone; the canonical score of 52/89 incorporates the two bug-fix re-runs from components 2 and 3.

components.label: main v0.1.15 sequential sweep
components.notes: Two tasks failed due to omega-cli bugs unrelated to agent capability: fix-code-vulnerability (exit 1 — omega dirty-tree check tripped by task repo at /app) and pytorch-model-recovery (exit 2 — clap rejected instruction string beginning with '- '). Both bugs fixed in v0.1.16; see docs/repl-and-substrates.html#session-i-error-analysis for full analysis.

components.label: v0.1.16 bug-fix re-run: fix-code-vulnerability
components.notes: Re-run after adding --allow-dirty to bench wrapper. Scored 1.000.

components.label: v0.1.16 bug-fix re-run: pytorch-model-recovery
components.notes: Re-run after adding allow_hyphen_values=true to omega-cli --instruction arg. Scored 1.000.

sweeps.reconstruction_note: All 89 tasks covered across four job directories. Take the latest-started trial per task across all four components.

components.label: main xhigh-76 parallel sweep
components.notes: 76 of 89 canonical tasks. xhigh effort used for tasks with >= 900 s budget; high effort for shorter tasks. 1 setup timeout (mailman).

components.label: missing-13 gap fill
components.notes: Gap-fill run for the 13 canonical tasks absent from the main parallel sweep.
